_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to turn pages in books and hold them the right way up.

_source.w: We are learning to turn pages in books and hold them the right way up.
y: Knowing how to handle a book helps us enjoy stories more easily.
Turning pages one at a time helps us follow what is happening.
These are the first steps to becoming a confident reader.
eg: We turn the pages of a picture book during shared reading time.
We hold a book the right way up and open it from the front.
We look at a digital text and swipe to the next page.
We notice which end of the book is the front and which is the back.
We sit with a partner and take turns turning pages.
We explore how a book opens and which way the pages go.
